summary: Iter 10 — Admin Console E2E (super-admin dashboard) GREEN. Backend: 17/17 new admin pytest cases pass (health, audit, risk/users, brokers/map, events, force-kill arm+release, 404 unknown user, 403 negative for 5 endpoints + kill, plus 4 regression endpoints). Frontend: full Playwright walkthrough — login as demo@algoforge.io → /admin via sidebar nav → 6 health cards render with live values (Mongo OK, Reconciler running, LLM Key OK, Users 24, Paper orders 339, Live brokers 0) → all 4 tabs switch cleanly → FORCE KILL on a non-admin trader row triggers prompt, accepts reason, toast success, row shows ARMED, button flips to RELEASE → ADMIN TRAIL tab confirms a new FORCE_KILL_SWITCH row with the reason text in summary → RELEASE reverts state → regression nav to Cockpit, Backtest, Paper, Trap, Audit, Brokers all load without errors.

design_issues.screen: Cockpit (and any page with a chart container)
issues: Recharts console warning: 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0'. Pre-existing; container collapses before measure. Not blocking but noisy.

action_items: (Optional) Fix Recharts width(-1)/height(-1) warning by ensuring chart parents have non-zero min-height before mount.

(Test-infra, not feature) backend/tests/backend_test.py TestIter9Refactor module-import tests fail standalone because pytest runs without ENCRYPTION_KEY env loaded — they need a conftest.py that loads /app/backend/.env before brokers/__init__.py imports Fernet. HTTP-level tests all pass; these are dotenv-loading issues only.
critical_code_review_comments: routers/admin.py admin_health() reads emergent_llm_key from os.environ at request time — fine, but consider caching reconciler state by reading a shared 'last_tick' timestamp instead of hard-coding 'running' (currently the card will always say running even if the lifespan task crashed).

services/admin_audit.record_admin_event() swallows all exceptions with a warning log. For high-severity actions (FORCE_KILL_SWITCH, PROMOTE_ROLE) consider re-raising so the API returns 5xx rather than silently dropping the audit row.

routers/admin.py admin_force_kill() upserts risk_limits.kill_switch without copying user-defined fields (max_drawdown_pct etc). The $set is fine (preserves them on update) but on first-time upsert the row will have only kill_switch+updated_at — acceptable, just call out.
Admin.jsx uses window.prompt() for the kill reason — works but blocks the React render loop and is hard to test. Replace with a shadcn AlertDialog for better UX and to allow E2E selectors.
Admin.jsx has no pagination on /admin/audit and /admin/events (next_cursor returned by backend is ignored). Will scale poorly past 50 rows.
test_credentials: demo@algoforge.io / Demo@123 (admin) + auto-created trader_<uuid>@test.io users per run for 403 tests (left in DB, cleaned-up on kill_switch release)
seed_data_creation: Per-run: 1 fresh trader user registered via /api/auth/register for 403 + force-kill round-trip. No persistent seed needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Admin console (iter 10) is GREEN end-to-end. Test file /app/backend/tests/test_admin_iter10.py is the canonical admin-endpoint suite — extend with new admin actions as they ship. NOTE: login response key is access_token (not token) — used across all auth fixtures. Force-kill writes both risk_limits.kill_switch and admin_events; the admin trail UI joins them by ts desc. WebSocket /api/ws/ticks is intentionally not connected (live ticks MOCKED per request) — the console errors are expected and not a regression. Pre-existing pytest-infra gap: backend_test.py TestIter9Refactor module-level imports need conftest.py that loads /app/backend/.env before brokers package imports (ENCRYPTION_KEY KeyError).
rca of the issue: N/A — no defects found. Initial pytest collection failed twice in this iteration only due to the test harness itself: (a) REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL not exported into the pytest subshell — fixed by also reading /app/frontend/.env inside the test file, (b) auth response key is access_token not token — fixed by updating the fixture. Both are local to /app/backend/tests/test_admin_iter10.py and resolved.
